title: Stonepeakが支援するエネルギー貯蔵プラットフォームのKingdom、初のプロジェクトファイナンス融資契約を締結
author: Na
description: Kingdom, a battery energy storage platform established by Stonepeak, has secured its first project finance loan agreement with Mitsubishi UFJ Bank for a 29MW BESS project in Okayama Prefecture, Japan. The project uses CATL batteries and represents the first financed asset among Kingdom's portfolio of 9 BESS projects totaling 479MW capacity secured through Japan's long-term decarbonization power auctions.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Secured lead arranger role for major BESS project finance deal, expanding its presence in Japan's energy transition financing and demonstrating confidence in the battery storage sector's growth potential.

title: CoreWeave Slides as Losses and Heavy CapEx Overshadow Revenue Beat
author: Timothy Fries
description: CoreWeave (CRWV) shares fell over 11% in premarket trading after reporting Q4 2025 earnings that beat revenue expectations but disappointed on losses and guidance. The AI cloud infrastructure provider posted $1.57B in revenue (110% YoY growth) but reported a loss per share of $0.89 versus analyst expectations of $0.49-$0.72. Q1 2026 guidance of $1.9-$2B fell short of Wall Street's $2.29B expectation. The company's aggressive capital expenditure plan, $21.37B in total debt, and negative free cash flow of $5.27B have raised concerns about sustainability.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock declined 11%+ on worse-than-expected losses ($0.89 EPS vs $0.49-$0.72 expected), disappointing Q1 2026 guidance ($1.9-$2B vs $2.29B expected), high debt levels ($21.37B), negative free cash flow (-$5.27B), and aggressive CapEx plans signaling significant cash burn ahead despite beating revenue expectations.
Mentioned as a client for CoreWeave's cloud computing build-out requiring an $8.5B loan, but no direct impact on Meta's operations or financials reported in the article.

title: Yen Weakens To July 2024 Lows, But Japanese Stocks Have Never Been Stronger
author: Piero Cingari
description: As the Japanese yen slides to its weakest level since July 2024 amid political uncertainty and speculation of a snap election under Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi, Japanese equities are surging to record highs. Exporters and financials are benefiting from the weaker currency, with the Nikkei 225 futures rallying 3.1% to fresh records. However, economists warn this combination of persistent yen weakness and rising bond yields is historically unusual and potentially unsustainable, with intervention risk increasing if the yen weakens further toward the 162-165 range.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: ETF has risen nearly 5% since the start of the year, outperforming the S&P 500 ETF, benefiting from the yen weakness and record highs in Japanese equities.
As the second-largest Nikkei component and a financial institution, it gained over 5% in recent trading, benefiting from the weaker yen and rising bond yields.
As the largest Nikkei component and major exporter, it surged more than 7% in Tokyo trading, directly benefiting from the weaker yen which improves export competitiveness.
Gained only about 1.6% since the start of the year, significantly underperforming the Japanese equity ETF, with no direct exposure to the yen weakness benefiting Japanese stocks.

title: STACK Infrastructure Secures AUD $1.3 Billion in Green Financing to Accelerate Expansion of MEL01 Hyperscale Campus in Melbourne
author: Preet Gona
description: STACK Infrastructure secured AUD $1.3 billion in green financing from five major banks to expand its MEL01 hyperscale data center campus in West Melbourne, Australia, demonstrating strong institutional confidence in its growth strategy.

title: These 2 Stocks Are Built for a ‘Higher-for-Longer’ Rates Regime
author: The Tokenist
description: The article discusses two stocks, Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group (MUFG) and Enbridge (ENB), that are well-positioned to benefit from a 'higher-for-longer' interest rate environment. MUFG is expected to profit from rising interest rates in Japan, while ENB's diversified portfolio and predictable cash flows make it resilient to higher rates.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article suggests that MUFG is poised to benefit from rising interest rates in Japan, as it will lead to greater profit margins on interest-sensitive assets like loans compared to deposit costs.

The article states that Enbridge's diversified portfolio and predictable cash flows make it resilient to higher interest rates, which could pose challenges for renewable energy companies that rely on favorable financing.
